cues.text: 第二十一課：你把這本書帶回去吧
cues.tr: Lesson 21: Take This Book Back With You

cues.text: 小華，你上次借給我的這本書真的很有幫助！
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: Xiao Hua, the book you lent me last time was really helpful!
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 是嗎？我記得那本書裡有一句話說「三人行，必有我師」，我覺得這句話特別有道理。
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: Really? I remember there’s a line in that book that says, “Among three people walking, there must be someone who can be my teacher.” I think this line is particularly meaningful.
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 對啊，每個人都有可以學習的地方。看了這本書後，我也開始更認真地觀察身邊的人。
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: Exactly, everyone has something we can learn from. After reading this book, I’ve also started observing the people around me more seriously.
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 沒錯！那本書不僅讓我學到很多知識，還改變了我的思考方式。我覺得它可能讓我更有耐心去理解別人。
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: That’s right! That book not only taught me a lot of knowledge but also changed the way I think. I feel like it’s made me more patient in understanding others.
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 我也是！以前我很容易對別人的缺點感到不滿，但現在我會嘗試去理解他們的處境。
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: Me too! I used to get easily frustrated with other people’s flaws, but now I try to understand their circumstances.
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 聽你這麼說，我真的很高興。既然這本書對你有幫助，你就把它帶回去再多看幾遍吧。
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: Hearing you say that makes me really happy. Since this book has been helpful to you, take it back and read it a few more times.
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 真的嗎？這麼好的一本書，你捨得給我帶回去？
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: Really? Such a great book—are you sure you’re okay with me taking it back?
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 當然了！如果能讓你進步，我怎麼會不捨得呢？
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: Of course! If it can help you improve, why wouldn’t I let you have it?
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 謝謝你，小華！我覺得這本書對我的影響真的非常大，有些道理如果我不經歷，不可能理解得這麼深刻。
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: Thank you, Xiao Hua! I feel like this book has had a huge impact on me. Some truths are hard to understand deeply without personal experience.
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 沒錯，有些東西真的是需要時間去體會。像這本書裡提到的事情，我也是看了好幾遍才慢慢領會的。
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: Exactly, some things really take time to fully grasp. Like the ideas mentioned in this book, I also had to read it several times before gradually understanding them.
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 那你會不會後悔把這本書借給我？
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: Then won’t you regret lending me this book?
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 哈哈，不會啦！我相信你會把這些道理應用到生活中去。
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: Haha, not at all! I trust that you’ll apply these lessons in your life.
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 那我一定會好好讀，把這些內容學到心裡。
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: I definitely will read it carefully and take these lessons to heart.
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 對對對，把它記在心裡，比什麼都重要。
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: Yes, yes, yes—keeping it in your heart is the most important thing.
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 其實我覺得，這本書裡的一些觀點如果不細心體會，可能不會懂得那麼多。
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: Actually, I think that some of the perspectives in this book might not be fully understood without careful reflection.
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 是啊，有些內容是要靠時間和經驗來體會的。
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: That’s true. Some content requires time and experience to truly appreciate.
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 話說回來，小華，你怎麼會知道這本書的？
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: By the way, Xiao Hua, how did you come to know about this book?
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 哦，是我朋友推薦給我的。他說這本書很適合我們這個年紀的人去學習一些人生的道理。
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: Oh, it was recommended by a friend. He said this book is perfect for people our age to learn some life lessons.
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 看來他也是你的「師」啊！三人行，必有我師，這句話真的沒錯。
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: Looks like he’s also your “teacher”! “Among three people walking, there must be someone who can be my teacher.” That saying is absolutely true.
cues.speaker: 小明

cues.text: 對啊，我也從他身上學到不少。他經常和我分享書籍和經驗。
cues.tr: Xiao Hua: Exactly, I’ve also learned a lot from him. He often shares books and experiences with me.
cues.speaker: 小華

cues.text: 小華，那如果我把這本書看完，可能不會再還給你了，哈哈！
cues.tr: Xiao Ming: Xiao Hua, if I finish reading this book, I might never return it to you, haha!
cues.speaker: 小明
